data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-7b88e3a7">
    <title>agent: auto_decompose_epic — wrap runPreClaimDecomposer for genuinely-undecomposed epics</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Genuinely undecomposed epics (IssueType==epic, totalChildCount==0, no closure-pending state) need automated decomposition into child task beads so ddx work can drain them. Today the only decomposition path is operator-invoked (bead-breakdown skill) or post-TooLarge recovery (cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go). FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires the work loop to auto-dispatch decomposition for this specific case (RC-6) with strict preconditions to avoid the v1-plan duplicate-child bug.

ROOT CAUSE
No handler exists that bridges the diagnoser's genuinely_needs_decomposition reason code to runPreClaimDecomposer (cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:48). The existing post-TooLarge hook (NewDecomposePostLadderExhaustionHook at recovery_decompose.go:107) gates only on TooLarge failure class, not on idle-scan diagnosis.

PROPOSED FIX
Add cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go exporting AutoDecomposeEpic(ctx, store, runner, rcfg, projectRoot, beadID) (DecomposeResult, error). It calls runPreClaimDecomposer (reused unchanged) after asserting every precondition. Preconditions (each must hold; first failing precondition produces a typed error AND increments the per-bead auto_decompose_attempted event count):
- store.Get(beadID).IssueType == "epic"
- totalChildCount(beadID) == 0 (use the same index ReadyExecutionBreakdown uses)
- bead is not currently in the EpicClosureCandidate bucket
- bead has no open dep_blocked_by or closed_or_missing_parent diagnoses (call the new Diagnose function on the bead; if it returns either, return ErrDepsBlockDecompose)
- bead carries no label in {manual-hold, no-auto-decompose, container}
- bead description is non-empty AND contains PROBLEM AND PROPOSED FIX sections AND has at least one acceptance criterion AND has at least one label matching ^(spec|area):
- per-bead event count of auto_decompose_attempted < 3
- bead has no active execution cooldown
- (operator override --no-auto-epic-decompose checked at the caller, not here)

On success: parent epic's execution_eligible is set to false (mirrors recovery_decompose.go behavior), child beads are created via store.Create, the auto_decompose_attempted event is emitted with body {child_ids: [...], cost_usd: N}.

On failure: SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, now+15m, "auto_decompose_failed", reason, baseRev), emit auto_decompose_failed event. On the 3rd cumulative attempt failure also emit auto_remediation_exhausted so the diagnoser surfaces it.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring this into the work loop (separate bead).
- Changing the runPreClaimDecomposer prompt (reused as-is).
- Surfacing the operator override flag (work-loop bead handles flag plumbing).

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. Depends on ddx-79e66725 (diagnose function) and ddx-e9f3adaf (RC-1 lifecycle fix — the closure-bucket check needs the correct classifier).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go exports AutoDecomposeEpic with the signature above.
2. TestAutoDecomposeEpic_HappyPath: bead is a valid undecomposed epic with proper description+AC+labels; AutoDecomposeEpic dispatches the decomposer (use the existing AgentRunner stub from recovery_decompose tests) and creates child beads.
3. TestAutoDecomposeEpic_PreconditionGates: one sub-test per precondition (non-epic, has children, in closure-candidate bucket, dep-blocked, manual-hold/no-auto-decompose/container label, missing description sections, missing spec:/area: label, attempt count at cap, active cooldown). Each returns the matching typed error and does NOT dispatch the agent.
4. TestAutoDecomposeEpic_FailureSetsCooldown: dispatcher returns an error; SetExecutionCooldown is called with status="auto_decompose_failed" and 15m TTL; auto_decompose_failed event emitted.
5. TestAutoDecomposeEpic_ThirdFailureEmitsExhausted: after three cumulative failures, an auto_remediation_exhausted event is also emitted.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestAutoDecomposeEpic -count=1 green.
7. cd cli && go test ./... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>kind:feat, area:agent, phase:2, spec:FEAT-010, spec:FEAT-004</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-2a1ece2b" base-rev="bf56ad62e1c0f245b6d0e9ad87fe952902227a95" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260521T105903-620a45b0"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
✓ Commit successful. AutoDecomposeEpic implementation with 8-gate precondition validation is complete. All 6 test functions pass:
- Happy path: creates child beads, sets execution_eligible=false
- Precondition gates: validates all 8 gates (epic type, child count, diagnoses, labels, description, attempt cap, cooldown)
- Fallback decomposition: handles invalid decomposer output
- Attempt cap and cooldown gates: enforce execution limits

Task complete.
